methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $370M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 13 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $9.2B.

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Quantum-Systems is a Series C defense and security company founded by Florian Seibel, currently valued at €3B with $370M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 13 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables, spanning 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies including Saronic, Anduril Industries, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $370M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $629M. Peer-median analysis across the 13-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.2B, with the peer range spanning $300M to $9.2B. Notable outliers include Saronic at $9.2B (Series D) and Chaos Industries at $4.5B (Series D), while earlier-stage peers cluster between $1.4B and $1.5B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $535M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $940M. The wide peer dispersion and stage heterogeneity within the comparable set—particularly the gap between Series B peers ($1.4B–$1.5B) and later-stage operators—introduce material uncertainty around appropriate valuation multiple. Refinement of this range depends on clarifying Quantum-Systems' specific revenue scale, growth rate, and technical differentiation relative to peers.
